title: Air-fryer bacon
subtitle: Crisp rashers without the pan or the spatter
excerpt: Back bacon or streaky bacon cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 6 to 8 minutes. No oil, no spatter, no standing over a hot pan. The air fryer renders the fat and crisps the bacon without any attention. Streaky goes crispy in 6 minutes; back rashers need 8.
sourceNotes: Bacon is one of the most commonly air-fried foods in Britain. The appeal is practical: the grease stays in the drip tray rather than covering the hob, and the result is evenly crisp without standing over the pan. Streaky bacon (UK) is equivalent to American bacon and crisps faster than back bacon because it has a higher fat-to-lean ratio.

content.text: Check at 6 minutes and decide from there. Bacon cook time in an air fryer varies more than almost any other ingredient because the thickness, fat content, and cut all differ between brands. Six minutes for streaky is a baseline: some brands will need 5, some 7. Check early rather than setting a timer and walking away.

items.name: Back bacon
items.prepNote: or streaky bacon, 2 rashers per person

content.text: Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Lay the {{back-bacon}} rashers in the basket in a single layer (slight overlapping is acceptable for back bacon, but lay streaky bacon flat). Cook at 200°C for 6 minutes for streaky or 8 minutes for back bacon. Check at 6 minutes: if the bacon has reached your preferred crispness, remove it. If you want it more crisp, continue in 1-minute increments. No flipping is needed. Drain on kitchen paper for 30 seconds before serving.

items.fix: Always preheat. Thick-cut bacon needs 10 to 12 minutes. Avoid overlapping rashers: they steam each other rather than crisping
items.cause: Air fryer not preheated, thick-cut bacon, or too much bacon in the basket at once
items.symptom: Bacon still floppy and under-cooked after 8 minutes

items.fix: Check from 4 minutes. Very thin streaky can burn quickly in high-powered air fryers
items.cause: Streaky bacon too thin, or air fryer running hot
items.symptom: Bacon very dark and brittle before the fat has rendered

items.fix: Place a small piece of bread or a tablespoon of water in the drip tray before cooking bacon. Clean the drip tray after every use to prevent grease buildup
items.cause: Bacon fat dripping onto the heating element

content.text: Air-fryer bacon is the fastest route to crisp rashers without standing over a hob. Use in a full English breakfast alongside air-fryer sausages and eggs, in a bacon sandwich with brown sauce, or as a topping for soups and salads. The grease stays in the machine rather than on the cooker.
